name: Cappuccino Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Butter a 10-inch springform pan.
3. Grind graham crackers to crumbs in a food processor.
4. Add nuts, sugar, and salt; chop coarsely.
5. Add butter and process until crumbs are evenly moistened.
6. Press crumbs into the bottom of the prepared pan.
7. Bake until edges begin to brown, about 15 minutes.
8. Cool.
1. In a heavy medium saucepan, bring whipping cream to a boil.
2. Reduce heat to low.
3. Add chopped semisweet chocolate; stir until melted.
4. Remove from heat and mix in light corn syrup.
5. Add butter (cut into pieces) one piece at a time, stirring until smooth.
6. Cool mixture to lukewarm, stirring occasionally.
7. Pour fudge over the cooled crust.
8. Refrigerate until firm, about 2 hours.
1. In a heavy medium saucepan, cook brown sugar and water over very low heat until thick.
2. With mixer running, gradually pour boiling syrup into egg yolks (do not scrape saucepan).
3. Continue to beat until yolk mixture is cool, about 15 minutes.
4. Reduce mixer speed to medium.
5. Mix in butter tablespoon at a time.
6. Add espresso dissolved in hot water and melted chocolate.
7. Spread buttercream over chilled fudge layer.
8. Cover pan loosely with waxed paper and refrigerate overnight.
To Serve:
- Run a small sharp knife around the sides of the pan.
- Carefully release the springform pan sides.
- May be served with whipped cream.
